title: Bridge Paper: Neural and phenotypic representation under the free-energy principle.
author: Frontier Science / Hermes T2

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Dependent Arising** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge thesis:** Predictive processing — free energy principle, Bayesian brain, active inference, neural causality.
**Phase context:** predictive coding, Bayesian brain, free energy, active inference, predictive processing

Paper: Neural and phenotypic representation under the free-energy principle.
DOI: 10.1016/j.neubiorev.2020.11.024
Abstract: The aim of this paper is to leverage the free-energy principle and its corollary process theory, active inference, to develop a generic, generalizable model of the representational capacities of living creatures; that is, a theory of phenotypic representation. Given their ubiquity, we are concerned with distributed forms of representation (e.g., population codes), whereby patterns of ensemble activity in living tissue come to represent the causes of sensory input or data.
